ops.title: Agent plane reverted: inline routing, one-shot triage/writers, retained round stamping
ops.body:
Commit 9dbae47 (ships as v0.16.0) reverts the v0.14.x present-handler agent plane and replaces it with inline routing plus one-shot delegates. Decision rationale — four confirmed production failures in one session (0f6bc33d on yasyf-home), each with a forensic root cause:

1. **Per-write stop/redispatch dance** — agent-origin writes were never teed into a parked handler's mailbox, so every main-session push/update-block forced a kill-and-redispatch; the "daemon supersedes the old handler" net the skill promised did not exist in code until eafec633 shipped it hours before the revert, and supersede only removes the TaskStop step — the redispatch-per-write remains.
2. **Round-2 re-presented settled blocks** — a whole-doc push re-stamped every top-level block into the current round by design; not a reducer bug, an authoring footgun.
3. **Handler silently disappeared** — normal post-submit stand-down with nothing re-dispatched; compounded by an upstream cc-interact bug where a custom Agent dispatch `name` is recorded as agent_type, silently breaking IsPresentHandler suffix matching (subscriptions, muting, `direct`) for every named dispatch.
4. **Submit raced a handler restart window** — the round's terminal event arrived while no handler was parked.

The replacement design: the main session routes events inline (informational = a ledger line; submit drained inline); actionable events dispatch a one-shot `present-triage` worker (opus-pinned, classify-only, zero board writes) for bursts or a single writer agent (session writing tier) for lone events. Carry-forward is removed entirely (round intent `--round current|new` stays; `--round new` freezes non-re-upserted blocks); `doc.replaced` carries a daemon-computed `retained` list — byte-identical top-level blocks keep their round, so a full push can never revive settled blocks (fix for failure 2, structural). Revision-writing ops (push/upsert/round/seeded start) serialize under a package mutex. Pack `host_api` stays pinned at identity 1 — sole-consumer doctrine: cc-present is the only host and consumer, so capabilities fold into identity 1 and the version never increments.

Reducer changes are triple-ported (Go/TS/Swift) in lockstep and pinned by shared fixtures, including two synthetic ones for the retained-no-prior-stamp edge and round-title persistence. Legacy payloads (carry keys, absent retained) replay with no error in all three ports — an error is permanent replay poison.

Known accepted edge: a pack block's nested field values compare whitespace-normalized but not key-order-normalized in retainedTopIDs, so hand-reordered nested keys re-stamp a settled pack block (safe direction only; documented in docs/contract.md).

Upstream flags, no action taken here: cc-interact's agent-start hook name/agent_type conflation is a live bug for any surviving agent-plane consumer; the uncommitted singleton/supersede WIP in the cc-interact checkout is unneeded after this revert (landed upstream as @29a3366 — the pin stays, cc-present just no longer wires Subscribe/MuteConsumer/AgentGreeting/SingletonSubscriber).
